name: Adam Schiff
stanceSummary: Schiff publicly supports emergency U.S. security assistance to Israel and has also called for including humanitarian aid for civilians in Gaza, reflecting a security-first but aid-inclusive position. He has taken funding from the Israel lobby.

timeline.event: Schiff said the U.S. must continue supporting Israel and criticized excluding humanitarian support for Gaza from House legislation.
Schiff urged House leadership to advance a supplemental package including aid for Israel and humanitarian funding for Palestinians in Gaza.

name: Adam Smith
stanceSummary: Adam Smith has supported Israel's security response after October 7 while also calling for humanitarian aid expansion and a political path including ceasefire and long-term two-state diplomacy. He has taken funding from the Israel lobby.

timeline.event: Smith stated Israel has a right to defend itself after Hamas attacks and emphasized long-term political solutions.
Smith called for immediate humanitarian aid scaling and ceasefire-linked strategy shifts in Gaza.

name: Alex Padilla
stanceSummary: Padilla has supported security packages that include Israel assistance while also advocating expanded humanitarian aid and renewed ceasefire diplomacy for Gaza. In this dataset, positive Israel-lobby funding keeps the stance label Pro-Israel.

timeline.event: Padilla backed large-scale humanitarian aid expansion for Gaza and renewed diplomatic efforts toward ceasefire.
Padilla said Senate passage of the national security package would deliver urgent humanitarian assistance to civilians in Gaza.

name: Amy Klobuchar
stanceSummary: Klobuchar condemned Hamas's October 7 attacks and emphasized U.S. support for Israel, while later backing broader aid legislation that also referenced humanitarian support for Palestinians. She has taken funding from the Israel lobby.

timeline.event: Klobuchar condemned Hamas attacks and said the United States stands with Israel.
Klobuchar described a bipartisan foreign-aid package as supporting key allies and humanitarian assistance, including aid for Palestinians.

stanceSummary: Biggs has taken a strongly pro-Israel security stance, repeatedly backing Israel's right to military self-defense and criticizing U.S. moves viewed as constraining Israeli operations. He has taken funding from the Israel lobby.

timeline.event: Biggs condemned rocket attacks on Israel and said he supports Israel's right to defend itself.
Biggs supported maintaining the U.S. embassy in Jerusalem and aligned with pro-Israel congressional efforts.

name: Angus King
stanceSummary: King has moved from supporting humanitarian-access measures to openly criticizing severe hunger conditions in Gaza and urging major policy change, while still participating in broader Senate debates on Israel-related security support. He has taken funding from the Israel lobby.

timeline.event: King urged expanded humanitarian corridors and distribution capacity for aid entering Gaza.
King said growing nutrition and starvation conditions in Gaza were unacceptable and called for changes in policy direction.

Grassley joined a Senate resolution condemning efforts to block or condition U.S. weapons transfers to Israel.
